title: Record surge in women patronage in KSRTC on Priyadarshini scheme’s inaugural day
content: On June 15, the KSRTC saw 13.29 lakh women passengers use its services, of which zero-fare tickets were issued to 7.83 lakh women. The KSRTC incurs a revenue loss of ₹2.20 crore on the first day following the launch of the scheme.

title: UP, Gujarat, Jharkhand, 10 other States record revenue surplus in FY25: CAG report
content: Of the 18 States that targeted revenue surplus, nine achieved the target, while Assam, Bihar, Chhattisgarh, Haryana, Himachal Pradesh, Karnataka, Maharashtra, Mizoram, and Telangana ended up being revenue deficit in 2024-25.

title: G7 Summit: Modi holds talks with Canada’s Carney, aims to visit Canada by year end, sign trade deal soon
content: The two countries have been negotiating a free trade agreement, with Commerce and Industry Minister Piyush Goyal visiting Canada last month with a delegation of over 100 industry representatives

title: Tripura Congress lodges complaint against CRPF over ‘assault of youth Congress members’
content: Senior party leaders have also demanded action against the CRPF jawans who allegedly used abusive language against the workers at a NEET protest rally

title: TMC MP Saugata Roy threatens breach of privilege motion against rebel MP Kakoli Ghosh Dastidar
content: Mr. Roy accuses Ms. Dastidar of breaking rules after she discussed Parliamentary standing committee meet with media; she blamed former TMC government for rampant child trafficking, marriage in W.B.

title: Participation in Yoga Day made mandatory for West Bengal Govt. staff
content: This will be the PM’s first public appearance in Bengal after he took part in the CM’s swearing in ceremony

title: West Bengal CID grills Abhishek Banerjee for third consecutive day
content: The Trinamool national general secretary is questioned in connection with an FIR lodged at the Bidhannagar police station for allegedly making provocative remarks in some speeches posted on social media

title: SC issues notice on plea seeking oversight of rehabilitation centres catering to children with disabilities
content: Observing that the matter raises important issues, the Bench said it required consideration “on priority”

title: IAF to participate in Exercise Pitch Black 2026 in Australia
content: Australian High Commissioner says the exercise is an opportunity to strengthen regional interoperability across the Indo-Pacific

title: Hindu prayers made mandatory in Chhattisgarh’s State schools; govt imposing RSS agenda, says Congress
content: Morning assembly will include the national anthem, national song, Deep Mantra, Saraswati Vandana, and Guru Mantra

title: TVK government’s White Paper puts Tamil Nadu’s debt at ₹13.18 lakh crore
content: The report says the headline debt figure of ₹10 lakh crore in 2025-26 captures only the State’s direct borrowings through market loans, institutional debt, and public account liabilities; it does not capture the borrowings of the PSUs, statutory boards, and special purpose vehicles that the State guarantees or implicitly supports

title: Delhi Police arrest seven, bust Pakistan-backed terror syndicate
content: Police say the network was functioning under the patronage of Pakistan’s ISI and was using Indians to further terror activities; police have arrested suspects from Uttar Pradesh and Punjab

title: India stares at 35% monsoon deficit
content: Central India faces 63% deficit as monsoon’s first pulse loses momentum at Mumbai’s outskirts; east, northeast face 43% shortfall; Centre directs crop-wise contingency plans, puts 150 to 200 districts under priority monitoring

title: Indian Ocean Rim Association ‘examines’ Canada’s application to become observer
content: Sanjiv Ranjan, IORA’s Secretary-General, said member states could benefit from Canada’s inclusion into the fold considering its expertise in varied maritime domains.

title: Teachers’ Recruitment Board to release mark list for filling 2,708 assistant professor posts by end of June
content: It is now awaiting clarification from the Higher Education Department on whether it can proceed with filling 1,292 additional assistant professor posts after the HC cleared the decks for it last month

title: World suffers from shortage of trust: PM Modi to G7 leaders
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi and U.S. President Donald Trump greet each other against a backdrop of strained India-U.S. ties; the two leaders are scheduled to meet on Wednesday

title: After nine years, two BSF men handed 20-year prison terms in Mizoram rape-acid attack case
content: The BSF personnel threw acid on one of the victims, disfiguring her face and causing partial loss of vision

title: NEET-UG re-exam: Telegram app restricted in India at NTA request
content: The agency said that several Telegram groups were advertising fake exam papers for the upcoming NEET exam and defrauding candidates willing to pay for them

title: Ritabrata says he has support of 65 Trinamool MLAs, dares rivals to take floor test
content: MLAs backing Mamata Banerjee not invited to the Business Advisory Committee meeting convened  by Speaker

title: ‘Not me’: Punjab CM denies that he is the person in a video that provoked the wrath of the Akal Takht
content: Bhagwant Mann claims he is the victim of “a malicious and politically motivated conspiracy” by SAD; Congress slams his confrontational response; BJP says he has no moral legitimacy to continue as CM

title: Farmers stage protest in Raichur
content: They are seeking release of pending dues for jowar procured from them at centres set up by the State govt.

title: Congress calls for time-bound judicial probe into Ram Mandir ‘missing donations’ row
content: U.P. Congress chief Ajay Rai says the probe by a Special Investigation Team into the matter is a farce, and, ‘The BJP-RSS have committed organised plunder at Shri Ram Janmabhoomi’

title: Rice, dhal and unanswered distress calls: Death of a mariner onboard Celestial Sea
content: Vessel’s problems encompass almost everything that Indian seafarers have faced in the last two-and-a-half months: Depleting supplies, problems with shipowners, distress calls not being attended to, rationing of food and water, long stressful days in the war zone, and the U.S. Navy’s hostile manoeuvres.

title: Karnataka plans to link voter ID for Gruha Jyothi reapplicants
content: Under the scheme, free electricity is being supplied every month subject to a maximum consumption limit of 200 units per month

title: Savarkar filed 10 mercy petitions, Hindutva ideologue’s grandnephew tells court
content: Satyaki Savarkar says that the language in these petitions did not indicate loyalty to the British regime, and filing clemency petitions was a standard procedure under the British government.

title: Chief Minister to launch Telangana Public School on Wednesday
content: The initiative is to bolster confidence in government schools; TPS at Arutla has facilities on par with corporate schools

title: Focus on working in rural areas: CM to medical graduates
content: A total of 103 students were awarded gold medals for scoring the highest marks in various disciplines; more than 72,000 were awarded degrees.

title: Watch: Why are prescriptions now mandatory for cough syrups? | Above the Fold | 16.06.2026
content: From new restrictions on cough syrups after child deaths, to the NTA’s move against Telegram and key updates from West Bengal, West Asia and the G7 Summit - Top stories of the day

title: A.P. CM Naidu highlights Andhra Pradesh as global investment hub in Singapore
content: The CM calls upon global investors to partner with Andhra Pradesh and become part of India’s next phase of economic transformation

title: Kerala’s move to intensify wild boar culling comes under fire
content: KFRI scientist calls for a science-based management strategy, warning that ‘indiscriminate killing’ could create ecological imbalances

title: Zubeen Garg’s last recorded duet with predeceased co-singer released
content: The song, ‘Xorogi Xopun’, is from the Assamese feature film Dipholu, to be released in theatres on August 21, 2026

title: Lok Sabha Speaker invites Mamata faction to ‘present case’ amid Trinamool split
content: Mamata Banerjee faction claims that the invitation was sent at 2 p.m. on June 15 asking party General Secretary Abhishek Banerjee to meet the Speaker by 4 p.m. the same day, while he was being interrogated by the Enforcement Directorate in Kolkata

title: Indian H-1B Worker Sues US Employer For Demanding Rs 94 Lakh From Him
content: The worker, identified as Rishikesh Raj Meesala, alleged that he was pressured into making payments by his Indian American employer Sai Jitender Kalagra.

title: Exam Irregularities Continue Despite Testing Body Reforms: Parliamentary Panel
content: The committee, headed by Digvijaya Singh, noted that such incidents had led to examination cancellations and caused significant stress for students preparing for highly competitive tests.

title: "Donor-Recepient" Ties Must End: PM Modi Backs Trust-Driven Relations At G7
content: PM Modi said energy, food, health, cyber and economic security have become so interconnected that building partnerships across nations is no longer optional but a necessity for global progress and prosperity.

title: A Few Hundred In 2016 To 2 Lakh In 2025: Tracking India's Startup Journey
content: Bharat Innovates 2026 received around 4,000 applications from deep-tech startups linked to higher education and technical institutions across India.

title: Phones Off, Uddhav Sena MPs Not Reachable, Focus Now Shifts To Delhi
content: Uddhav Thackeray and other senior party leaders are making personal efforts to persuade the MPs. Some parliamentarians have been described as "not reachable".

title: Bengaluru Couple Moved In Together 6 Months Ago. Then He Strangled Her
content: The couple had reportedly met through Instagram and had been living together in Malleswaram for the past six months.

title: Body Found Stuffed In Mattress, Delhi Couple Arrested Months Later
content: A special team conducted extensive enquiries in labour colonies and slum clusters across several areas of Delhi-NCR and scrutinised footage collected from more than 300 CCTV cameras.

title: Telegram CEO Pavel Durov Reacts To India Ban Until NEET Re-Test
content: The move, which covers the period of the NEET-UG 2026 re-examination scheduled for June 21 and its immediate aftermath, follows recommendations from the National Testing Agency (NTA).

title: Fly Without Meal, Pay Less: Air India Launches Lower Fares On Select Routes
content: The airline in a press release said it was introducing the 'Basic' fare category, under which customers will have greater control over how they travel and what they pay for.

title: Rs 13 Lakh Crore Debt Burden On Tamil Nadu: White Paper By TVK Government
content: The White Paper says Tamil Nadu's revenue deficit has reached a historic high of Rs 78,324 crore, while fiscal deficit stands at Rs 1.33 lakh crore.

title: Russian Warship Fires 'Warning Shots' At British Yacht In Channel: Report
content: It came days after UK commandos intercepted and boarded a suspected Russian shadow fleet vessel in the same part of the Channel, in the first such UK-led operation.

title: 'Nuclear Plants Were Hacked, Working To Prevent It': Global Watchdog
content: The concern is compounded by the growing digitisation of nuclear plants and the increasing reliance on complex computer systems.

title: Mumbai Stops Water Supply To Pools, Construction Sites Amid Crisis
content: The move comes as the total stock in lakes supplying water to Mumbai has depleted to a critical level of 10.35 per cent as of June 16, the BMC said in a circular on Tuesday.

title: G7 Summit 2026 LIVE Updates: PM Modi Holds Bilateral Meet With UK Prime Minister Keir Starmer
content: G7 Summit 2026 LIVE Updates: This is India's 13th participation in the G7 as a guest nation and PM Modi's seventh consecutive appearance at the summit.

title: Key Issues On Agenda For PM Modi, Donald Trump's Bilateral Tomorrow
content: The biggest agenda of the meeting is expected to the India-US bilateral trade agreement. While the deal is not expected to be finalised during the meeting, some technical discussions may take place, US officials have indicated.

title: Why This American Covid Lab Company Is Giving Millions Of Dollars In Payouts
content: At its peak, GS Labs handled between 20 and 30 per cent of the rapid testing market in states such as Minnesota and Washington.

title: PM Modi, Trump's G7 Handshake Ahead Of Bilateral, First Meet In 16 Months
content: Prior to this interaction, the last time the two leaders met face-to-face was in February 2025 at the White House in Washington, DC, shortly after Trump's second inauguration.

title: Ghost Ships Are Hiding A Bigger Risk Than Expected. They Are Floating Hazards
content: The US Coast Guard's cyber teams say these ship owners often ignore both physical safety rules and digital security checks, leaving the vessels vulnerable.

title: Rs 47-Crore Multi-State Fake GST Racket Busted In Assam
content: In a joint operation that included raids across Assam, Bihar and West Bengal, a Special Task Force (STF) and GST officials busted the network accused of committing fake Input Tax Credit (ITC) fraud.

title: 'Very important': India coach reveals how Rohit remains crucial to Gill
content: Ahead of the second ODI against Afghanistan, India spin bowling coach Sairaj Bahutule backed Rohit Sharma, calling him a "champion cricketer" and highlighting his influence on ODI captain Shubman Gill and the team. Bahutule also praised KL Rahul's versatility and form, saying the batter can adapt to any situation and remains a key member across formats.

title: '3-letter sentence' that cost California billions of dollars as Elon Musk left the State
content: A resurfaced 2020 tweet from a California labor leader has been blamed by social media users for Elon Musk's departure from the state, costing it billions. While the tweet is a focal point, Musk's exit was driven by years of clashes with officials and a desire for a more business-friendly environment.

title: 329 million children in India exposed to extreme heat: UNICEF report
content: A UN report reveals 92% of Indian children face extreme heat, with 89 million exposed to heatwaves. While India has Heat Action Plans, they lack child-specific measures. India's high hazard exposure score, especially for extreme heat and air pollution, highlights the urgent need for targeted interventions to protect young lives.

title: 'If everyone in this country ... ': CJP founder Abhijeet Dipke clears stance on fighting polls
content: Cockroach Janta Party founder Abhijeet Dipke ruled out electoral politics, emphasizing that citizens shouldn't need to contest elections to demand basic rights. Ahead of a nationwide demonstration against the NEET-UG paper leak, Dipke criticized the Centre for its lack of engagement with the youth and demanded the Union Education Minister's resignation.

title: US used Iran-style covert transfers to move 90 million barrels of oil out of Gulf: Report
content: The U.S. has reportedly established a covert offshore oil-transfer network near the Strait of Hormuz, mirroring Iran's tactics to maintain Gulf crude exports. This operation, involving ship-to-ship transfers off Oman and the UAE, aims to circumvent Tehran's blockade of the vital waterway. While enabling continued exports, the network's reliance on clandestine methods raises concerns about its vulnerability and the U.S.

title: Dell CEO Michael Dell makes one of largest public university donations in US history
content: Michael Dell has gifted a staggering $750 million to the University of Texas at Austin, propelling the creation of a groundbreaking AI-native hospital and research campus. This substantial donation, adding to his family's legacy of support, will also bolster scholarships, advanced computing, and vital medical research, underscoring Dell's enduring connection to his alma mater.

title: ‘Weapon more powerful than any nuke’: Iran can shut Strait of Hormuz again, US intel warns
content: Iran now possesses the capability to effectively shut down the Strait of Hormuz, a critical global oil and gas route, according to US intelligence. This development comes despite an upcoming peace agreement aimed at reopening the waterway. Concerns persist over Iran's military arsenal and its potential to use the strait as leverage in future disputes, even as diplomatic efforts continue.

title: 'Can always come to Moscow': Russia's open invitation to Zelenskyy for ‘serious’ dialogue
content: Ukraine's President Zelenskyy is welcome in Moscow for serious peace talks, the Kremlin stated, though no official communication channels exist. This comes amid renewed diplomatic efforts, including US President Trump's discussions with Zelenskyy and anticipated visits by American representatives to Russia. European leaders urged a firmer stance against Moscow at the G7 summit.

title: Call drops, frozen payments and buffering screens are the new traffic jams of India's cities
content: A TRAI assessment reveals inconsistent mobile network performance on Delhi-NCR's Metro and Namo Bharat corridors, impacting commuters' daily digital lives. Despite advancements, seamless connectivity remains a challenge due to underground infrastructure and moving users, highlighting the growing need for integrated telecom solutions in urban transit.

title: US senator says Tesla benefited from govt support, Elon Musk replies
content: Elon Musk has refuted claims that his companies, Tesla and SpaceX, owe their success to government aid, stating such incentives represent less than 2% of their value. The debate ignited between Senators Mike Lee and Ed Markey over taxpayer contributions to Musk's wealth. Musk asserted that removing EV tax credits actually boosted Tesla's market share.

title: 'Shameless': Oppn targets PM Modi for meeting Trump after US strikes killed 3 Indian sailors
content: In the wake of Prime Minister Modi's encounter with Donald Trump at the G7, opposition factions have come out strongly against him, highlighting the heartbreaking deaths of three Indians due to a US military strike. The Congress party circulated a video purportedly showing Trump dismissing Modi, and the AAP denounced the cordiality of their interaction as 'embarrassing.

title: Amin Hajee recalls career struggles after Lagaan and how Aamir Khan helped him recover
content: A quarter century after Lagaan became one of Indian cinema's most celebrated films, actor and filmmaker Amin Hajee has made a candid admission about the impact the movie had on his career. While the Aamir Khan-starrer opened doors for many associated with it, Hajee says it "destroyed" him in an unexpected way. Hajee revealed that his wife Charlotte was concerned about him sitting at home and turning down projects after Lagaan.

title: Will Lionel Messi play today? Argentina Star’s injury update ahead of Algeria match
content: Lionel Messi is expected to start for Argentina against Algeria in the FIFA World Cup 2026 opener after recovering from a minor hamstring issue. Lionel Scaloni has provided a positive fitness update as Argentina prepares to begin its title defense in Kansas City. Messi’s availability also puts him on course to make history with a record sixth FIFA World Cup appearance.

title: NFL trade rumors: Chiefs, Ravens linked to Falcons' $15M tight end
content: The Atlanta Falcons placed a franchise tag on star player Kyle Pitts but it seems his name is still a regular concerning trade rumors ahead of the new season even as it has sparked chaos about his future in the team. Amid this, the Baltimore Ravens and the Kansas City Chiefs have emerged as potential landing spots for the star player.

title: Taylor Swift reportedly found 'real' love with Travis Kelce after dating struggles
content: Taylor Swift and Travis Kelce are gearing up to tie the knot next month as rumors swirl about a grand wedding in the Madison Square Garden in New York City next month. While the high profile couple have now been together for close to three years, there have been many doubts about their relationship. But it seems Taylor Swift thinks differently.

title: How Qatar, not Pakistan, became the real power broker in US-Iran deal
content: While Pakistan sought the spotlight in its diplomatic endeavours, it was Qatar’s subtle, low-key mediation that ultimately held the key to the US-Iran ceasefire. Leveraging its unique relationship with Tehran and access to the American administration, Qatar's patient and discreet shuttle diplomacy triumphed over more boisterous efforts that were ridiculed globally.

title: Cues from Lucknow nets: Rohit faces chin music, Iyer rehearses the pull
content: Rohit Sharma, Shubman Gill, Shreyas Iyer and Ishan Kishan put in the hard yards at the Ekana Stadium ahead of the second ODI against Afghanistan. While Kuldeep Yadav bowled an extended spell, Iyer worked extensively on the pull shot that proved to be his undoing in Dharamsala. India will take on Afghanistan on Wednesday in Lucknow.

title: Watch: PM Modi and Donald Trump exchange greetings at G7 Summit ahead of expected talks
content: Prime Minister Narendra Modi and US President Donald Trump had a brief interaction at the G7 Summit in France, their first in 16 months. The leaders were seen shaking hands and engaging in conversation, with Trump also patting Modi's arm. They were seated next to each other during a summit session.

title: Water crisis deepens in Mumbai: BMC halts supply to pools, construction sites
content: Mumbai faces severe water scarcity as lake levels drop to 10.35%, prompting the BMC to impose a 20% cut on industrial and commercial supply, suspend water to construction sites and swimming pools, and limit bottling plants. These measures, effective June 17, add to existing citywide restrictions amid a delayed monsoon.

title: Rupee snaps two-day rally, settles 2 paise lower at 94.60 against US dollar
content: The rupee dipped slightly to 94.60 against the dollar on Tuesday, ending a two-day gain. Despite falling crude oil prices and hopes for a US-Iran peace deal, foreign investors selling Indian equities pressured the currency. Analysts anticipate a near-term downward trend for the rupee, with support from easing oil prices.

title: 'Huge plus for any team': Sunil Gavaskar identifies India's next X-factor
content: Nitish Kumar Reddy's impressive bowling, particularly a match-turning yorker to dismiss Rahmanullah Gurbaz, has earned high praise from Sunil Gavaskar.  The all-rounder's developing bowling prowess and batting ability offer significant tactical flexibility to the Indian ODI team, making him a valuable asset. His recent IPL success further solidifies his all-round credentials.

title: Pakistan-linked terror-crime network busted in Delhi-NCR, seven held
content: Delhi Police has apprehended seven individuals in connection with a Pakistan-backed terror-crime module. The operatives are accused of smuggling arms and narcotics from Pakistan, conducting reconnaissance of potential targets, and sharing information with handlers across the border. The network allegedly operated under the patronage of Pakistan's ISI.

title: 3 BLO visits, one missed form, voter list trouble? Telangana SIR puts urban voters on alert
content: Telangana is undertaking a Special Intensive Revision of electoral rolls. Booth-level officers are tasked with visiting every household to verify voter status. Challenges like locked houses and migrant populations may lead to voters being marked absent. Eligible voters can seek inclusion during the claims and objections period. Political parties are urging vigilance to prevent the deletion of genuine voters.

title: AMCA and the future of air warfare: What it takes to build a fifth-generation fighter
content: India has launched the next phase of its Advanced Medium Combat Aircraft (AMCA) program, aiming for its first indigenous fifth-generation stealth fighter. This ambitious project, led by private industry, seeks to close the stealth gap with China and Pakistan, marking a significant shift in India's defense manufacturing.

title: FIFA World Cup 2026 Recap: A Day of Only Draws after 68 years
content: Four draws in one day, Spain’s 2,500-pass drought, Cape Verde’s LinkedIn hero, Bielsa’s anti-modelling rant, and a night where football remembered that stalemates can still have stories.

title: US stocks today: Dow hits record high as easing oil prices boost Wall Street
content: Indian markets mirrored global optimism as US stocks neared record highs, fueled by falling oil prices and hopes for a US-Iran peace deal. SpaceX's impressive market debut and gains in financial and chip stocks also boosted investor confidence. All eyes are now on the Federal Reserve's upcoming policy decision and guidance from its new chair.

title: 'SL players should also be educated': India coach breaks silence on Sooryavanshi controversy
content: India A spin bowling coach Sairaj Bahutale addressed a post-match altercation involving 15-year-old Vaibhav Sooryavanshi after their Super Over loss to Sri Lanka A. Bahutale called the incident unfortunate, emphasizing the need for education on player conduct for both teams. He expressed confidence that Sooryavanshi, a young representative of India, would learn from the experience and avoid repeating it.

title: Bobby Deol wishes son Aryaman on his birthday: 'Love you most'
content: Bollywood actor Bobby Deol took to social media on Tuesday, June 16, to pen a heartwarming birthday post for his eldest son, Aryaman Deol, who turned 25. The Animal actor shared a striking selfie of the father-son duo twinning in black, captioned: "Hey my Aryaman, my angel...Happy Birthday, Love you most." The post garnered affectionate wishes from industry peers, including actor Rahul Dev and businessman Raj Kundra. Born on June 16, 2001, to Bobby and Tanya Deol, Aryaman typically stays out of the limelight, having made his first major public appearance at the IIFA Awards in 2018.

title: Land of the free kick: For Iran, it's 90mins of football and six hours of border crossing
content: Iran's football team drew 2-2 with New Zealand in Los Angeles amidst significant logistical challenges and political undertones. Players faced immediate travel to Mexico post-match, with coach Ghalenoei criticizing the lack of recovery time. The tournament has seen multiple teams complain about stringent security and restrictive measures, impacting the athlete experience.

title: SpaceX soars past Amazon in market value after IPO frenzy; options trading begins
content: SpaceX shares soared over 14% Tuesday, briefly surpassing Amazon and nearing Microsoft's valuation, reaching $2.85 trillion. Investor excitement surged with new options trading, despite analysts warning of extreme volatility and questioning the lofty valuation. The company also plans a $60 billion acquisition.

title: 'Foolish to write him off': Ex-India captain backs veteran for ODI WC 27
content: Former captain Kris Srikkanth backs Ravindra Jadeja for the 2027 World Cup, urging selectors not to dismiss the all-rounder despite recent form. He also believes Rohit Sharma's ODI future hinges on performance, not age, as India shapes its squad for the next global tournament.  The team currently leads Afghanistan 1-0.

title: Air India launches 'basic fare' option without complimentary meals on select domestic flights
content: Air India has introduced an optional 'Basic fare' on select domestic routes, excluding complimentary meals to cater to price-sensitive travelers. This new option offers a more unbundled travel choice, while existing fare categories with meals and bundled benefits remain available. The airline will evaluate customer feedback during this pilot phase to decide on its continuation.

title: Stock market: Which are top 10 gainers and losers on NSE & BSE today? Check list
content: Indian equities surged for a third day, with Sensex and Nifty climbing on positive global cues, foreign investment, and easing crude oil prices following a reported US-Iran peace deal. IT stocks led the gains, with HCL Tech announcing a significant investment. Broader markets also participated in the rally, reflecting widespread optimism.
